intro: Sarcomphalus mexicanus, also known as Mexican snail vine or Mexican snail climber, is a perennial plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it a valuable remedy for treating various conditions such as fever, respiratory issues, and skin irritations. Bioactive constituents of Sarcomphalus mexicanus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to take advantage of its therapeutic benefits, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
